query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Illicit', 'Forbidden by law, rules, or custom', 'Scholars noted a striking illicit between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Illimitable', 'Impervious to limitation, without limit', 'Critics argued that the plan might illimitable the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Illiterate', 'Unable to read and write', 'Critics argued that the plan might illiterate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Illogical', 'Contrary to logic, lacking sense or sound reasoning', 'Critics argued that the plan might illogical the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Illuminant', 'Something that illuminates', 'Her illuminant became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Illuminate', 'To light up or clarify', '“The professor illuminated the complex theory with a simple analogy.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Illumine', 'To illuminate', 'Critics argued that the plan might illumine the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Illusion', 'Anything that seems to be something that it is not', 'Critics argued that the plan might illusion the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Illusive', 'Subject to or pertaining to an illusion', 'The author hoped the new policy would illusive tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Illusory', 'Resulting from an illusion, deceptive, imaginary, unreal', 'Her illusory became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Illustrious', 'Highly distinguished, renowned, famous', 'The passage presents a illustrious portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imaginary', 'Existing only in the imagination', 'The essay highlights the imaginary of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imbibe', 'To drink (used frequently of alcoholic beverages)', 'The author hoped the new policy would imbibe tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imbroglio', 'A  misunderstanding, disagreement, etc', 'Her imbroglio became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imbrue', 'To stain (in, with, blood, slaughter, etc )', 'Over time, the community learned to imbrue its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imbue', 'To stain an object completely with some physical quality', 'Critics argued that the plan might imbue the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imitation', 'The act of imitating', 'Her imitation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imitator', 'One who imitates or apes another', 'Her imitator became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immaculate', 'Free from spot or stain, immaculately clean', 'The author hoped the new policy would immaculate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immaterial', 'Having no matter or substance', 'The passage presents a immaterial portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immature', 'Not fully formed or developed, unripe', 'What seemed immature at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immeasurable', 'Impossible to measure', 'The author hoped the new policy would immeasurable tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immense', 'Extremely large or great', '“The ocean’s immense depth makes it difficult to explore.”', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immerse', 'To put under the surface of a liquid, to dunk', 'Over time, the community learned to immerse its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immigrant', 'Of or relating to immigrants or the act of immigrating', 'Critics argued that the plan might immigrant the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immigrate', 'To move into another country to stay there permanently', 'Critics argued that the plan might immigrate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Imminent', 'About to happen', 'What seemed imminent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immiscible', '(of two or more liquids) that are not mutually soluble', 'Scholars noted a striking immiscible between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immoderate', 'Excessive or lacking, not consistent', 'Over time, the community learned to immoderate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immolate', 'To sacrifice', 'The author hoped the new policy would immolate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immoral', 'Not moral, inconsistent with rectitude or good morals', 'The passage presents a immoral portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immortalize', 'To bestow unending fame upon, perpetuate', 'Critics argued that the plan might immortalize the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immovable', 'Incapable of being physically moved, fixed', 'The reviewer described the tone as immovable, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immune', 'Exempt, not subject to', 'The essay highlights the immune of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immunity', 'The state of being insusceptible to something', 'Critics argued that the plan might immunity the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immure', 'To cloister, confine, imprison, to lock up behind walls', 'Over time, the community learned to immure its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Immutable', 'Unchanging over time or unable to be changed', '“The laws of physics are considered immutable.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impair', 'To weaken, to affect negatively', 'The author hoped the new policy would impair tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impalpable', 'Incapable of being perceived by touch, intangible', 'The passage presents a impalpable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impart', 'To communicate or make known', '“The mentor imparted valuable wisdom to his students.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impartial', 'Treating all rivals equally; fair', 'The passage presents a impartial portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impassable', 'Incapable of being passed over, crossed, or negotiated', 'The passage presents a impassable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impasse', 'A situation from which there is no escape, deadlock', 'The essay highlights the impasse of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impassible', 'Not allowing passage over, through, along, etc', 'The essay highlights the impassible of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impassion', 'Filled with intense emotion or passion, fervent', 'Scholars noted a striking impassion between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impassive', 'Showing no emotion', 'What seemed impassive at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impatience', 'Restlessness and intolerance of delays', 'Her impatience became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impeccable', 'Faultless, flawless, irreproachable', 'The reviewer described the tone as impeccable, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impecunious', 'Having little or no money', 'The reviewer described the tone as impecunious, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Impede', 'Prevent or hold back', 'The essay highlights the impede of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
